narrative: Your law school application was denied because our predictive model, which assesses the likelihood of first-time success on the bar exam, estimated a 56.1% chance that you would not pass it right away. This prediction draws from your academic record, test scores, family background, and other details like planning to attend full-time, placing you in a group where the model sees elevated risk compared to typical applicants. The most influential factor pushing toward this failure prediction was your Year 3 school decile ranking of 1, which is notably lower than the average of around 5.5 and signaled weaker performance relative to peers. Next in importance, your undergraduate GPA of 3.40 stood out positively—above the typical 3.21 average—and pulled the prediction toward expecting you to pass, counteracting some concerns. Your Year 1 decile of 1, also well below the average of about 5.7, added further weight toward the failure side, reinforcing patterns the model associates with bar struggles. While your LSAT score of 29.5 is below the usual 36.7, and your middle family income bracket aligns with averages, these played lesser roles here. As a Black male applicant, your profile reflects strengths like that solid undergrad record that the model valued, yet the early and later school rankings dominated the decision, setting you apart from students whose consistent high deciles often lead to approvals. This isn't a judgment on your potential—many overcome similar starts—but the model prioritizes bar passage stats to protect program outcomes. To move forward, consider retaking the LSAT to boost your score, strengthening your high school deciles through additional coursework or explanations in reapplications, and targeting schools with holistic reviews that weigh your unique background. Building a narrative around your resilience as a full-time student from a middle-income family could also highlight upsides. Reach out to admissions advisors for feedback, and explore prep programs tailored for bar success to shift future predictions in your favor. With targeted steps, you can reapply stronger next cycle.
